hero_subtitle: SFTY connects and analyzes transaction flows across regulated entities to provide authorities with evidence-ready insights and structured supervisory workflows for digital asset oversight.
trust_card_1_value: Market participants, licensing data and risk profiles
trust_card_2_value: Wallets, transactions and exposure across the market
trust_card_3_value: Originator and beneficiary data checked for completeness
trust_card_5_value: On-premise or private cloud for sensitive data
trust_card_6_value: Supervisory outputs, evidence and regulatory reports
problem_title: Supervisory insight breaks across transaction flows
problem_description: Regulators struggle to connect transaction data with licensed entities and their risk context when information remains dispersed across multiple providers. This fragmentation slows supervisory analysis and complicates evidence gathering for effective oversight and reporting.
problem_point_1_value: VASP activity, licensing data and risk signals remain scattered
problem_point_2_value: Alerts and investigations depend on disconnected manual processes
problem_point_3_value: Decisions are harder to explain without connected logs and case history
problem_point_4_value: Regulatory teams lack one shared operating layer for supervision
solution_description: SFTY links transaction data with licensed entities, risk contexts, and supervisory cases to create an integrated operating layer that accelerates oversight and preserves evidence for regulatory review and reporting.
workflow_title: From market signals to supervisory action
workflow_description: SFTY connects transaction data, risk alerts and VASP profiles through case workflows that support evidence collection, review and regulatory reporting for authorities.
workflow_step_1_description: Wallet, VASP, Travel Rule or OSINT signal appears
workflow_step_2_description: SFTY classifies and routes the signal for review
workflow_step_3_description: A supervisory case opens with context & evidence
workflow_step_4_description: Teams record a decision, request or supervisory response
workflow_step_5_description: Evidence, actions and outcomes stay available for review
why_sfty_title: Not another analytics tool. One supervisory layer
why_sfty_description: SFTY structures fragmented transaction and entity data into connected cases, evidence, and reports, enabling clear supervisory oversight across regulated digital asset flows.
why_sfty_card_1_description: See activity across VASPs, wallets, entities and risk signals
why_sfty_card_2_description: Connect participants, structures, links and supervisory records
why_sfty_card_3_description: Move from alerts to investigations, actions and reporting
why_sfty_card_4_description: Understand not only single risks, but the condition of the market
authority_description: SFTY provides regulators with secure deployment, evidence-grade logging, and access governance to support controlled supervisory workflows and procurement-ready implementation.
authority_card_1_description: Deploy on-premise, in private cloud or inside a controlled government environment
authority_card_2_description: Preserve activity, decisions, case history and reporting evidence
authority_card_3_description: Control who can view, review, escalate and act across teams and agencies
authority_card_4_description: Support phased implementation, documentation, integrations and governance review
ask_sfty_placeholder: Ask SFTY how transaction flows support supervisory decisions
ask_sfty_chip_1_message: Explain how wallet and transaction data link to regulated entities
ask_sfty_chip_2_message: Show how evidence-grade logs preserve transactional audit trails
ask_sfty_chip_3_message: Describe how multi-provider outputs connect in oversight workflows
ask_sfty_helper_text: AI assistant for supervisory, deployment and compliance questions
faq_description: Clear answers on VASP supervision, sovereign deployment, evidence logging and regulatory workflows.
faq_1_question: What is SFTY for financial authorities?
faq_1_answer: SFTY provides a supervisory layer that connects licensing, entity data, wallets, transactions and risk signals into a connected, evidence-ready view for financial authorities and central banks.
faq_2_question: How does SFTY analyze regulated transaction flows?
faq_2_answer: SFTY links wallet, transaction, VASP and Travel Rule data, enabling authorities to monitor flow patterns, risk exposures and cross-entity interactions within supervised markets.
faq_3_question: How does SFTY preserve supervisory evidence?
faq_3_answer: SFTY preserves case histories, decision logs and supporting data with audit logs for secure, defensible record-keeping in regulated supervision environments.
faq_4_question: Can SFTY integrate multiple data providers?
faq_4_answer: Yes. SFTY orchestrates outputs from KYT, KYC, KYB, Travel Rule and analytics providers into structured supervisory workflows without replacing existing systems.
faq_5_question: Does SFTY replace existing analytics tools?
faq_5_answer: No. SFTY complements existing analytics by consolidating provider outputs into structured cases, evidence and reports, enhancing overall supervisory effectiveness.
final_title: Review transaction flow supervision with SFTY
final_description: Explore how SFTY connects transaction data, regulated entities and supervisory records into structured, evidence-ready workflows for authorities.
footer_description: Supervisory and compliance control layer for digital asset markets, businesses and regulated institutions.
footer_contact_title: For supervisory, compliance and deployment discussions.
footer_legal_notice: This page is for informational purposes only and does not constitute legal, regulatory or compliance advice. SFTY deployment depends on jurisdiction, client requirements and applicable laws.
